nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q8.alt: Question 8. Based on information in the article, what can the reader conclude is the most likely reason Yo-Yo was able to play a public recital when he was only five years old? Choices: A: Yo-Yo realized that he enjoyed performing for an audience’s reaction. B: Yo-Yo was ready to perform because of his father’s teaching. C: Yo-Yo had finally found an instrument that he enjoyed playing. D: Yo-Yo knew he would perform the music well because of his excellent memory.

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q9.alt: Question 9. Which statement best describes the relationship between Yo-Yo Ma and his parents? Choices: A: They support Yo-Yo’s early desire to become a cello player. B: They all enjoy listening to different types of music. C: They hope Yo-Yo’s choice of instrument will be the piano. D: They are all talented enough to become famous musicians.

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q10.alt: Question 10. Which detail from the article best shows how Yo-Yo connects with people when he performs? Choices: A: “Yo-Yo Ma has been playing the cello since he was a young boy. He gave his first concert at the age of five.” (paragraph 1) B: “Yo-Yo Ma rocks his cello from side to side. He draws the bow across its strings.” (paragraph 2) C: “He throws his head back. His eyes close, his glasses reflecting the bright lights of the stage.” (paragraph 2) D: “The audience grows quiet. Soon they are drawn into a story he tells using his cello” (paragraph 3)

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q11.alt: Question 11. Which detail from paragraph 6 best supports the meaning of the phrase “as natural as breathing” in the same paragraph? Choices: A: “surrounded by music” B: “trained as an opera singer” C: “played piano and violin” D: “records of classical music”

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q12.alt: Question 12. Why are paragraphs 6 and 7 important to the article? Choices: A: They suggest that Yo-Yo’s musical talents come naturally to him. B: They show how Yo-Yo’s parents greatly influence his passion for music. C: They explain why Yo-Yo has more interest in the cello than the violin. D: They describe how Yo-Yo quickly learns to play because of his teacher.

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q13.alt: Question 13. Which central idea is supported by details in paragraphs 12 and 13? Choices: A: Family members are necessary for success. B: Learning something new can prove challenging. C: Small problems rarely cause people to feel stress. D: Breaking large tasks into smaller parts helps learners.

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q14.alt: Question 14. Read this claim from paragraph 15. Dr. Ma said Yo-Yo played “like a shining star.” Which phrase from paragraph 15 best supports this claim? Choices: A: “had a good memory” B: “concentrated better than most” C: “could play three Bach suites by heart” D: “gave his first public recital at the University of Paris”

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q22.alt: Question 22. Which statement best expresses First Woman’s relationship with the First People? Choices: A: She hopes her people will not become discouraged. B: She is focused on bringing new types of light to her people. C: She believes her people should be happy with the light they have. D: She is frustrated that her efforts are not appreciated by her people.

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q23.alt: Question 23. Which phrase best supports the meaning of “illuminate” as used in paragraph 1? Choices: A: “too far away to bring heat” (paragraph 1) B: “They would give more light” (paragraph 3) C: “They flicker on and off” (paragraph 4) D: “the people brought her a slab of quartz” (paragraph 6)

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q24.alt: Question 24. How does the narrator mainly develop the character of First Woman? Choices: A: by describing her actions B: by referring to her visit to Fire Mountain C: by sharing her thoughts D: by showing her working with First Man

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q25.alt: Question 25. Which sentence best represents the turning point in the story? Choices: A: “Eventually, First Woman had an idea for a different way to send light to Earth.” (paragraph 6) B: “On the rim, they placed bird feathers—cardinal, lark, and eagle—to fly it through the sky and spread the rays of heat and light in the four directions.” (paragraph 7) C: “At that moment, two ancient, wise men stepped forward and offered to help.” (paragraph 11) D: “Once the Sun and the Moon were following their paths through the sky, the people returned to where First Woman had carved the wheels.” (paragraph 12)

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q26.alt: Question 26. Which detail best expresses a theme of being determined? Choices: A: “So First Woman sent Glowworm, Fox Fire, Lightning Beetle, and Firefly to the four corners.” (paragraph 3) B: “She consulted Fire Man on his glowing mountain.” (paragraph 4) C: “It took a lot of hard work and a lot of help . . .” (paragraph 6) D: “. . . they worked with their flint knives, chisels, and stone hammers to shape the stars.” (paragraph 13)

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q29.alt: Question 29. What is the meaning of the word “bask” in paragraph 1? Choices: A: to enjoy B: to hide C: to ignore D: to swim

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q30.alt: Question 30. Paragraph 10 is important to the article because it Choices: A: describes why a biologist “loves to look at turtles” B: questions why turtles are rarely “all the same shape” C: suggests that some turtles have round “dome shells” D: states that various shells are better for “different turtles”

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q31.alt: Question 31. According to scientists, turtles have existed on this planet for more than 220 million years. How does the author support this idea in the article? Choices: A: by explaining that turtles belong to “the same family as snakes and lizards” (paragraph 3) B: by referring to a “fossil and other clues” that show how shells have changed (paragraph 4) C: by explaining that it is possible to “see from the ridges how much the turtle has grown” (paragraph 7) D: by referring to the “flat shape and leathery shell” that help alligator snapping turtles swim quickly (paragraph 11)

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q32.alt: Question 32. Which phrase from paragraph 14 best supports the meaning of the word “camouflage” in the same paragraph? Choices: A: “a lighter color on their plastron” B: “looking down into the water” C: “blends in with the dark water” D: “predators swimming below”

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q33.alt: Question 33. According to information in the section “What Shape Is Your Shell?”, how do some turtles catch their prey? Choices: A: by making holes in the sand B: by remaining still and using their tongues C: by pulling their legs into their shells D: by having brown shells and yellow on their bellies

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q34.alt: Question 34. The diagram helps the reader better understand the article by showing Choices: A: the location of different parts of a turtle’s shell B: the size of the scutes found on a turtle’s shell C: how the bridges connect the shell to the plastron D: how the turtle is able to avoid different predators

nysed-ela-2025-g5-mc-q35.alt: Question 35. Which detail would be most important to include in a summary of the article? Choices: A: Turtle muscles attach to shells in the same way human muscles attach to bones. B: Different parts of the turtle shell work together to form a protective barrier. C: Keratin can be found in both turtle shells and human hair and nails. D: Scutes protect the turtle’s bones and provide color to the shell.
